subcodes.title: Words to True, Esslemont, spoken on 1919-11-20
subcodes.first_line: Bahá'u'lláh frequently predicted that there would be a period when irreligion and consequent anarchy would prevail; the chaos would be due to too great liberty among people who were not fit for self-government.
subcodes.subjects: @power and sovereignty of God; omnipotence of God, @corruption and decline of Islam; of the Shi'ih, @present and future calamities; war; universal convulsion, @justice and wrath of God, @nationalism; love of country
